vitals.aid_stations: Rest stops spaced at average of 25 miles apart

course_description.overview: A fully supported 3-day circumnavigation of Puerto Rico covering approximately 375 miles. The event features three pelotons divided by ability level, accommodating an international field of cyclists.

key_segments.name: Island Circumnavigation
key_segments.description: Fast-paced route that circumnavigates the entire island of Puerto Rico with rest stops spaced approximately 25 miles apart

course_description.difficulty_narrative: The event is designed as a fast-paced circumnavigation with three ability-based pelotons, suggesting moderate to challenging pacing. The coastal route and island terrain present varied conditions across the three-day format.

logistics.getting_there: Event starts in Old San Juan (Fortaleza Street corner of Tetuan Street, San Juan 00901). Participants should arrange their own transportation to Puerto Rico.
logistics.accommodation: Not included in event registration. Participants must arrange their own lodging in San Juan or nearby areas.
logistics.registration_details: Event runs February 15-17, 2026. Registration details available through event organizers. Single-day participation options may be available based on similar regional events.
logistics.bike_logistics: Fully supported event with support vehicles throughout. Mechanical support available during the event.
logistics.local_considerations: Coastal Caribbean climate with potential for heat and humidity. Rest stops provide hydration and nutrition. Event is organized for international cyclists.

final_verdict.one_liner: A well-organized Caribbean circumnavigation offering scenic coastal riding with solid logistics and international participation.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Cyclists seeking a supported multi-day road event with scenic Caribbean views. Suitable for intermediate to advanced riders comfortable with 125+ mile daily distances. International cyclists looking for a unique destination event.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for those prioritizing scenic coastal riding and full support logistics. The three-peloton structure accommodates varying abilities. Value depends on accommodation costs and travel expenses to Puerto Rico.
pro_tips: Book accommodation in San Juan well in advance for February dates
Arrive early to acclimate to Caribbean heat and humidity
Choose your peloton carefully based on honest assessment of your pace
Bring sun protection and plan for coastal wind conditions
